page: Microsoft Azure EC2eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV6 family. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

For storage, the server includes 118 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage configurations. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC2eds_v6 provides a low-entry point for memory-intensive workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation rather than shared resources. The combination of dedicated compute, high memory density, and local storage makes this instance suitable for small-scale database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure EC2eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV6 family, designed for workloads requiring a high memory-to-core ratio. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an Intel x86_64 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Storage capabilities include 118 GB of local disk space alongside support for Premium Storage. The instance does not include GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-bound tasks, delivering dedicated compute resources and local storage without the overhead of larger instance sizes. It is suited for small-scale database deployments, caching systems, and lightweight memory-intensive enterprise applications.
